input.background: A citizen claims that the intelligence of children in a particular community is above average. To test this claim, he selects a simple random sample consisting of n=9 children from this population. Scores of the intelligence test typically vary according to a Normal distribution with mean μ=100 and standard deviation σ=15. The sample produces these scores {116,128,125,119,89,99,105,116,118}. Thus, x̄=(116+128+⋯+118)/9=112.8.
input.question: Test statistic.

output.answer: The SE_x̄=σ/√n=15/√9=5 and the z_stat=(x̄-μ)/SE_x̄=(112.8-100)/5=2.56. Figure 9.6 is a schematic of the sampling distribution of x̄ and the z_stat under H0.

sample_scores.description: The intelligence test scores of the sample of 9 children.

sample_mean.description: The mean of the intelligence test scores of the sample.

population_mean.description: The average intelligence test score of the population, assumed to be the mean of the Normal distribution.

population_std_dev.description: The standard deviation of the intelligence test scores in the population, assumed to be the standard deviation of the Normal distribution.

sample_size.description: The number of children in the sample.
